titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward M. Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers
Burns (Edward) Collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes and Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 14 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 01:00

did.unittitle: Edward Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Edward M. Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers primarily contains correspondence, writings, realia and print materials collected by Edward M. Burns and relating to the lives and work of Alice B. Toklas, Gertrude Stein, and their associates.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 25-26 (audiocassettes, microcassettes, and videocassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 27 (phonograph records): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 28 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into seven series: I. Correspondence, 1906-2016. II. Writings, 1896-2010. III. Artwork and ephemera, 1934-1980. IV. Photographic prints, 1906-1989. V. Publications, pamphlets, and other papers, 1915-2022. VI. Audiovisual materials, 1935-1995. VII. Computer media, 1942-2020.

bioghist.head: Edward M. Burns
bioghist.p: Edward M. Burns (1944-) is a professor of English at William Paterson University. Burns has authored and edited numerous articles, critiques, and collections about Gertrude Stein and her contemporaries.

bioghist.head: Gertrude Stein (1874-1946)
bioghist.p: Gertrude Stein (1874-1946) was an American writer and supporter of the arts. She spent her adult life living and working in Paris, France with her life partner, Alice B. Toklas. A biographical timeline is provided in the register for the Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas papers (YCAL MSS 76).

bioghist.head: Alice B. Toklas (1877-1967)
bioghist.p: Alice B. Toklas (1877-1967) lived and worked with her life partner, Gertrude Stein, in Paris, France. Alice and Gertrude were supporters of artists and literary figures of the early 20th century such as Pablo Picasso, Ernest Hemingway, and Henri Matisse. A biographical timeline is provided in the register for the Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas papers (YCAL MSS 76).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward M. Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edward M. Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein papers contains correspondence, writings, print materials, photographs, artwork, audiovisual materials, realia, and other papers by, to, or relating to Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein collected by Edward M. Burns spanning the years 1896 to 2020.

Series I, Correspondence, is divided into three sub-series: outgoing correspondence from Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas, outgoing correspondence from others, and incoming correspondence. The outgoing correspondence from Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas subseries contains primarily a series of letters from Alice B. Toklas to Louise and Redvers Taylor. Outgoing correspondence from others includes material from Thornton Wilder and Daniel-Henry Kahnweiler. Incoming correspondence pertains primarily to communications received by Edward M. Burns including persons such as Cecil Beaton, Robert Indiana, Daniel-Henry Kahnweiler, Michel Leiris, Sonia Orwell, Sir Francis Rose, Louise and Redvers Taylor, Isabel and Thornton Wilder, and others. This subseries also contains other third-party correspondence to Ulla Dydo, Edgar Allen Poe, and Thornton Wilder.

Series II, Writings, is arranged into two subseries: writings by Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein, and writings by others. Writings by Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein contains multiple editions of Steins work, and most notably, a commonplace book kept by Alice B. Toklas from 1896 to 1901. Writings by others contains published and unpublished material by individuals including Edward Burns, Leon Katz, Michel Leiris, Carl Van Vechten, and Isabel Wilder.

Series III, Artwork and ephemera, contains drawings, paintings, posters, and household items once owned by Alice B. Toklas, Gertrude Stein, and Carl Van Vechten. Among the visual artists represented in this series are Sir Francis Rose, Francisco Riba-Rovira, and Robert Indiana.
Series IV, Photographic prints, contains prints of Alice B. Toklas, Gertrude Stein, and other individuals including friends.
Series V, Publications, pamphlets, and other papers, includes print publications, playbills, catalogs, critiques, and clippings collected by Edward M. Burns.
Series VI, Audiovisual material, includes videocassettes, audiocassettes, and microcassettes relating to Gertrude Stein, Alice B. Toklas, and Isabel Wilder.
Series VII, Computer media, includes CDs and a DVD.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Edward M. Burns collection of Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 25 (microcassettes): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into three sub-series: Outgoing correspondence from Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas, 1906-1966. Outgoing correspondence from others, 1923-1988. Incoming correspondence, 1930-2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence from Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas, Edward Burns, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials in this series are arranged into two sub-series: Writings by Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein, 1896-2020. Writings by others, 1966-2005.

did.unittitle: Writings by Alice B. Toklas and Gertrude Stein

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by creator's last name.

arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by creator's last name.

arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains artwork and ephemera related to or collected by Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas.

scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints and other photographic formats depicting or relating to Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas.

accessrestrict.p: Box 27: (record albums): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged into four sub-series: Publications, 1919-2022; Playbills, pamphlets, and catalogs, 1915-2022; Critiques and other papers, 1946-2019; and Clippings, 1915-2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains publications, playbills, pamphlets, catalogs, clippings, and other papers or works created by or relating to Gertrude Stein, Alice B. Toklas, Edward Burns, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged chronologically by publication date.

arrangement.p: Materials are arranged chronologically by publication date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audiovisual materials including videocassettes and audiocassettes relating to Alice B. Toklas, Gertrude Stein, and their friends.

did.unittitle: "Beds" by Leon Katz; "Nurturing Alice", "Dear Bosie", "Wedding Night"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Audio distortion, hum, and hiss are present.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Sorrel Hays: Gertrude Stein Songs from "Mapping Venus"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note included with the audiocassette reads: Dear Mr. Burns, Here are two settings of Gertrude Stein texts, drawn from the radio opera I made for Westdeutscher Rundfunk Cologne. The stage opera version "Mapping Venus" is now in piano/vocal score, a two hour work and I'm lookng for a production company. Meanwhile - I thought you may enjoy hearing how I set the words and come next week to the concert if you can. Best regards, Sorrel Hays

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note associated with these microcassettes indicate they are Ulla Dydo's tapes of Isabel Wilder talking about letters between Thornton Wilder and Gertrude Stein.
